summary: High‑energy second set with monster Playing In The Band and tight jams
blurb: The second set roars with a monster Playing In The Band, seamless transitions and soaring jams. Jerry’s wah‑infused guitar shines while Phil delivers an “absolute animal” bass line. Sugar Magnolia, Greatest Story Ever Told and Deal are standout moments.
review: November 18, 1972 at Houston’s Hofheinz Pavilion captured one of the band’s most electrifying late‑1972 outings. The first set rolls through a solid mix of Americana and folk‑rock, but it’s the second set that defines the night – a tour‑defining stretch that builds from a punchy Bertha into a thunderous Playing In The Band, followed by an inspired Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo and a jubilant Sugar Magnolia. Jerry Garcia’s wah‑infused tone on PITB is repeatedly called “incredible,” and his lead work on Greatest Story Ever Told glows with the late‑’72 confidence that fans cherish. Phil Lesh prowls like an “absolute animal” on bass, driving the jams while occasionally nudging the mix a touch high, a point noted by some listeners. Bob Weir locks in perfectly with Bill Kreutzmann’s tight drumming, adding melodic depth throughout. Keith Godchaux’s tasteful fills pepper the set, though his mix can edge forward at times. The recording quality is excellent – the Miller soundboard capture is clear and well‑balanced, with only minor pitch drift that can be corrected, making it the definitive source for this show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings praise the same high‑energy second set, especially Playing In The Band, Sugar Magnolia and Greatest Story Ever Told. Multiple reviewers highlight Jerry’s phenomenal guitar work and Phil’s powerhouse bass, while noting only minor mix quirks. Consistent positive language across sources warrants a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear, well‑balanced soundboard capture with only minor pitch issues that can be corrected; outperforms other sources.

key_highlights: Monster Playing In The Band that caps a high‑octane second set
Tight transitions and driving jams throughout the second half
Standout Sugar Magnolia and Greatest Story Ever Told performances
Keith’s tasteful keyboard fills (mix slightly forward) add color

band_performance.Jerry: Wah‑infused tone and big grin; his playing on Playing In The Band is called “incredible,” with attractive late‑1972 phrasing throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Described as an “absolute animal” – pounding, exploratory bass that drives the jams; a few note it was a touch loud but also praised for pure‑gold entrances.
band_performance.Bob: Locked in perfectly with Bill’s drums, adding melodic depth and tight rhythm work to the improvisations.
band_performance.Keith: Tasteful fills and overall keyboard work are praised; mix is a bit high, which can affect guitar balance but adds color to the set.
band_performance.Billy: Tight lock‑in with Bob, driving the rhythmic flow of the set; his drumming receives compliments for tightness and energy.
